text: unpopular opinion: your posts aren't growing your X account. your replies are.
small account math: your post reaches your 300 followers. your reply on a 100k account's post reaches theirs. replies are the only free distribution you have.
the problem isn't knowing this. it's writing the 15th good reply of the day. that's where everyone quits.
so i built a chrome extension that drafts the reply inside X's reply box. pick a tone — funny, sarcastic, sincere, one-liner — one click, done.

it's called WittyWing. free for 3 days, 10 replies/day, no card needed.

https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/wittywing/nanmlpbedgfahicpngbhalhjokieoipa

deleted the api key setup screen from my extension. activation doubled.

every onboarding step you remove is worth more than any feature you add.

8 tones. 1 click. 0 dashboards.

the whole product lives inside X's reply box. no separate app, no copy-pasting between tabs.

consistency beats brilliance on X.

one great reply does nothing. twenty decent replies a day, every day, compounds into an audience.

credits that never expire shouldn't be a differentiator.

yet here we are.
manifest v3 service workers die silently. if your extension's background script 'randomly stops working', it isn't random. design for it being killed at any moment.

the difference between 'i should reply more' and actually doing it is about 25 seconds per reply.

i built WittyWing to delete those 25 seconds. link in bio.
